title: Te Whakamahi i ngā Rauemi o Te Taiao | Harnessing Nature's Materials: A Ngāti Kahungunu Approach
subject: Technology
culturalContext: Students will explore the traditional harvesting and processing of harakeke (flax) from the banks of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku (the Mangakōtukutuku Stream), connecting to Ngāti Kahungunu practices of sustainable resource management and the pūrākau (legend) of the taniwha who protects these waters
learningObjectives: Identify and safely harvest harakeke using traditional tikanga (protocols) from designated areas along Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
Create a functional item (kete or whāriki) using at least three traditional raranga (weaving) techniques demonstrated by local experts
Analyze the environmental impact of modern synthetic materials compared to traditional harakeke products using a sustainability matrix
Present a digital portfolio documenting the harvesting, creation process, and cultural significance of their completed work

activities.title: Te Hīkoi Mātauranga | Knowledge Walk
activities.description: Guided visit to Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku with local kaumātua to learn proper harvesting protocols (tikanga), identify suitable harakeke, and understand the cultural significance of this resource in Ngāti Kahungunu history

activities.title: Te Whakatika i te Harakeke | Preparing the Flax
activities.description: Hands-on workshop using traditional tools (pāraerā) to strip, soften, and prepare harakeke fibres for weaving, learning the proper techniques for different weaving applications

activities.title: Te Mahi Raranga | The Art of Weaving
activities.description: Multi-session workshop with local weavers (tohunga raranga) learning basic techniques (whenu, aho, tapahi) to create either a small kete (basket) or whāriki (mat) using traditional patterns

activities.title: Te Arotake Taiao | Environmental Analysis
activities.description: Comparative life cycle assessment of harakeke products versus synthetic alternatives, examining water usage, biodegradability, and cultural sustainability through digital mapping tools

activities.title: Te Whakaaturanga | Digital Exhibition
activities.description: Creation of digital portfolios using Adobe Spark or similar tools to document the entire process, including cultural reflections, technical learning, and environmental analysis for whānau presentation

resources: Local Ngāti Kahungunu weavers and kaumātua from Mangakōtukutuku marae
Traditional harvesting tools (pāraerā) and weaving materials
Te Papa Tongarewa online collection 'Te Hei Tiki: The Evolution of Māori Style'
Regional Council water quality monitoring data for Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku

tasks: Completed functional harakeke item demonstrating at least three traditional techniques
Digital portfolio documenting process, cultural learning, and environmental analysis
Oral presentation to whānau explaining the cultural and technical aspects of their work

nzcAlignment: Technological Practice: Planning for practice, brief development, outcome development and evaluation
Technological Knowledge: Understanding technological modelling and products
Nature of Technology: Characteristics of technology and characteristics of technological outcomes
Māori dimensions: Tikanga-ā-iwi, te reo Māori, and mātauranga Māori
